title: Contributing to UtilsGGSV
text: Thank considering contribution UtilsGGSV! following guidelines help get started quickly ensure contribution fits smoothly project.

title: Contributing to UtilsGGSV
text: Please respectful constructive interactions. project follows Contributor Covenant Code Conduct.

title: Contributing to UtilsGGSV
text: Open issue GitHub Issues include: minimal reproducible example (use reprex::reprex() possible). output sessionInfo(). clear description expected vs. actual behaviour.

title: Contributing to UtilsGGSV
text: Open issue labelled enhancement describe: problem want solve. sketch proposed API (function name, parameters, return value). enhancement belongs package rather user code.

title: Contributing to UtilsGGSV
text: Fork repository create feature branch main: Install development dependencies: Make changes, following style guide . Add update tests tests/testthat/test-{function}.R. Run full check locally confirm passes ERRORs, WARNINGs, unexpected NOTEs: Push branch open pull request main.
code: git checkout -b my-feature renv::restore() devtools::document() devtools::test() devtools::check()

title: Contributing to UtilsGGSV
text: Follow conventions described .github/instructions/ (R coding standards, testing guidelines, package development workflow). Use snake_case function variable names. Prefix internal (non-exported) helpers . (e.g. .my_helper()). Document exported functions roxygen2. Every @param, @return, @examples section required. leave trailing whitespace end lines blank lines. Use package::function() notation rather attaching packages library() inside package code.

title: Contributing to UtilsGGSV
text: Create R/{function_name}.R complete roxygen2 header. Run devtools::document() regenerate NAMESPACE man/. Create tests/testthat/test-{function_name}.R tests. Update _pkgdown.yml include new function reference index. Mention addition NEWS.md # UtilsGGSV (development version).

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: UtilsGGSV provides utility functions plotting R using ggplot2. vignette demonstrates main functions package.
code: library(UtilsGGSV) library(ggplot2) library(magrittr) theme_set(cowplot::theme_cowplot())

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: ggcorr function creates scatterplots correlation coefficients overlaid. primary function package useful visualizing relationships groups measurements.

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
code: set.seed(3) response_vec_a <- rnorm(5) response_tbl <- data.frame(   group = rep(letters[1:3], each = 5),   response = c(     response_vec_a,     response_vec_a * 1.2 + rnorm(5, sd = 0.2),     response_vec_a * 2 + rnorm(5, sd = 2)   ),   pid = rep(paste0("id_", 1:5), 3) )  ggcorr(   data = response_tbl %>% dplyr::filter(group %in% c("a", "b")),   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid" )

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: can display multiple correlation coefficients simultaneously:
code: ggcorr(   data = response_tbl %>% dplyr::filter(group %in% c("a", "b")),   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = c("spearman", "pearson") )

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: comparing two groups, function creates pairwise comparisons:
code: ggcorr(   data = response_tbl,   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = "kendall" )

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: concordance correlation coefficient useful assessing agreement two methods:
code: ggcorr(   data = response_tbl %>% dplyr::filter(group %in% c("a", "b")),   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = "concordance",   abline = TRUE,   limits_equal = TRUE )

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: Text placement, font size, visual elements can customized:
code: ggcorr(   data = response_tbl %>% dplyr::filter(group %in% c("a", "b")),   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = c("spearman", "pearson", "concordance"),   abline = TRUE,   limits_equal = TRUE,   coord = c(0.4, 0.17),   font_size = 3,   skip = 0.04,   pval_signif = 2,   est_signif = 2,   ci_signif = 2 )

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: text placement remains consistent even axes transformed:
code: ggcorr(   data = response_tbl %>% dplyr::mutate(response = abs(response + 1)^4),   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = "spearman",   abline = TRUE,   limits_equal = TRUE,   trans = "log10",   skip = 0.06 )

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: axis_limits function helps manage axis limits, particularly useful forcing equal limits axes expanding axis coordinates.
code: data("cars", package = "datasets")  p0 <- ggplot(cars, aes(speed, dist)) +   cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy") +   geom_point() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +   labs(title = "Axes unadjusted") +   labs(x = "Speed", y = "Distance")  p1 <- axis_limits(   p = p0,   limits_equal = TRUE ) +   labs(title = "Axes limits equal")  p2 <- axis_limits(   p = p0,   limits_expand = list(     x = c(0, 50),     y = c(-10, 200)   ) ) +   labs(title = "Axes limits expanded")  cowplot::plot_grid(p0, p1, p2)

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: add_text_column function adds text annotations plots consistent positions, regardless underlying axis transformations.
code: data_mod <- data.frame(x = rnorm(mean = 1, 10)^2) data_mod$y <- data_mod$x * 3 + rnorm(10, sd = 0.5) fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = data_mod) coef_tbl <- coefficients(summary(fit)) results_vec <- c(   paste0(     "Intercept: ",     signif(coef_tbl[1, "Estimate"][[1]], 2),     " (",     signif(coef_tbl[1, 1][[1]] - 2 * coef_tbl[1, 2][[1]], 3),     ", ",     signif(coef_tbl[1, 1][[1]] + 2 * coef_tbl[1, 2][[1]], 3),     "; p = ",     signif(coef_tbl[1, 4][[1]], 3),     ")"   ),   paste0(     "Slope: ",     signif(coef_tbl[2, "Estimate"][[1]], 2),     " (",     signif(coef_tbl[2, 1][[1]] - 2 * coef_tbl[2, 2][[1]], 3),     ", ",     signif(coef_tbl[2, 1][[1]] + 2 * coef_tbl[2, 2][[1]], 3),     "; p = ",     signif(coef_tbl[2, 4][[1]], 3),     ")"   ) )  p <- ggplot(   data = data_mod,   aes(x = x, y = y) ) +   geom_point() +   cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy")  add_text_column(   p = p,   x = data_mod$x,   y = data_mod$y,   text = results_vec,   coord = c(0.05, 0.95),   skip = 0.07 )

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: plot_cluster_* family functions helps visualise characteristics clusters identified unsupervised learning method. examples use palmerpenguins dataset: first apply k-means clustering bill flipper measurements, explore clusters using function.
code: set.seed(42) penguins <- palmerpenguins::penguins penguins <- penguins[complete.cases(penguins[   c("bill_length_mm", "bill_depth_mm", "flipper_length_mm", "body_mass_g") ]), ] vars_penguin <- c(   "bill_length_mm", "bill_depth_mm", "flipper_length_mm", "body_mass_g" ) km <- kmeans(scale(penguins[vars_penguin]), centers = 3) penguins$cluster <- paste0("K", km$cluster)

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: plot_cluster_heatmap function creates heat map tile shows percentile median value variable cluster. percentile compared empirical cumulative distribution function (ECDF) variable across observations cluster. Clusters variables ordered along axes via hierarchical clustering.
code: plot_cluster_heatmap(penguins, cluster = "cluster", vars = vars_penguin)

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: plot_cluster_density function visualises, variable, cluster’s observations distributed relative overall population. density argument controls shown: "" (default): overall density plus per-cluster density curves. "overall": overall density per-cluster median lines. "cluster": one density curve per cluster.

previous_headings: Cluster-Specific Plots > Density Plots with plot_cluster_density
title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
code: plot_cluster_density(   penguins,   cluster = "cluster",   vars = vars_penguin,   n_col = 2 )

previous_headings: Cluster-Specific Plots > Density Plots with plot_cluster_density
what: Overall density with cluster median lines
title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
code: plot_cluster_density(   penguins,   cluster = "cluster",   vars = vars_penguin,   density = "overall",   n_col = 2 )

previous_headings: Cluster-Specific Plots > Density Plots with plot_cluster_density
title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
code: plot_cluster_density(   penguins,   cluster = "cluster",   vars = vars_penguin,   density = "cluster",   n_col = 2 )

previous_headings: Cluster-Specific Plots > Density Plots with plot_cluster_density
title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: density "" "cluster", scale argument controls cluster curves scaled relative overall density: "max_overall" (default): cluster density rescaled maximum equals maximum overall density. Y-axis values reflect overall density. "max_cluster": rescaling; y-axis determined tallest curve. "free": rescaling (equivalent "max_cluster").
code: # scale = "max_cluster": natural scale for all curves plot_cluster_density(   penguins,   cluster = "cluster",   vars = vars_penguin,   density = "both",   scale = "max_cluster",   n_col = 2 )

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: plot_cluster_scatter function creates biaxial scatter plot observations coloured cluster median centroids overlaid. supports four dimensionality reduction methods via dim_red argument: "none", "pca", "tsne", "umap".

previous_headings: Cluster-Specific Plots > Scatter Plot with plot_cluster_scatter
what: PCA projection (default for more than two variables)
title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
code: plot_cluster_scatter(   penguins,   cluster = "cluster",   vars = vars_penguin,   dim_red = "pca" )

previous_headings: Cluster-Specific Plots > Scatter Plot with plot_cluster_scatter
title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: exactly two variables needed directly axes:
code: plot_cluster_scatter(   penguins,   cluster = "cluster",   vars = c("bill_length_mm", "flipper_length_mm"),   dim_red = "none" )

previous_headings: Cluster-Specific Plots > Scatter Plot with plot_cluster_scatter
title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: t-SNE available Rtsne package installed:
code: plot_cluster_scatter(   penguins,   cluster = "cluster",   vars = vars_penguin,   dim_red = "tsne" )

previous_headings: Cluster-Specific Plots > Scatter Plot with plot_cluster_scatter
title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: UMAP available umap package installed:
code: plot_cluster_scatter(   penguins,   cluster = "cluster",   vars = vars_penguin,   dim_red = "umap" )

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: plot_cluster_mst function computes minimum-spanning tree (MST) clusters, using Euclidean distance cluster median profiles. Clusters positioned two dimensions via classical multidimensional scaling (MDS). variable, separate plot shows cluster node filled ECDF-standardised percentile cluster’s median — colour scale used plot_cluster_heatmap. default named list plots returned; supplying n_col n_row returns combined cowplot::plot_grid figure variable names labels.
code: plot_cluster_mst(   penguins,   cluster = "cluster",   vars = vars_penguin,   n_col = 2 )

title: Getting Started with UtilsGGSV
text: get_trans function returns transformation objects scales package. adds support additional transformations like higher roots asinh:
code: get_trans("log10") #> Transformer: log-10 [1e-100, Inf] get_trans("asinh") #> Transformer: asinh [-Inf, Inf] get_trans("root_cube") #> Transformer: root_3 [-Inf, Inf]

title: Copilot Instructions for UtilsGGSV
text: R package providing utility functions ggplot2 plotting.

title: Copilot Instructions for UtilsGGSV
text: Type: R Package Purpose: ggplot2 plotting utilities Dependencies: ggplot2, cowplot, scales, dplyr, tibble, purrr Tests: testthat (edition 3)

title: Copilot Instructions for UtilsGGSV
text: R/ - Source code (exported functions, . prefix internal) man/ - Auto-generated docs (edit directly) tests/testthat/ - Tests (use test-{function}.R naming) vignettes/ - Package vignettes

title: Copilot Instructions for UtilsGGSV
text: plot_cluster_heatmap - Heat map ECDF-standardised variable values per cluster. Tiles show percentile cluster’s median value compared observations outside cluster. plot_cluster_density - Density plot variable per-cluster median lines. Plots overall density overlays coloured vertical line cluster median.

title: Copilot Instructions for UtilsGGSV
text: ggcorr - Scatter plots correlation coefficients overlaid axis_limits - Force equal axis limits expand axis coordinates add_text_column - Add column text annotations plot save_plot / ggsave2 - Save plots disk get_trans - Return scales transformation objects name

title: Copilot Instructions for UtilsGGSV
text: Make minimal, surgical changes fix issues Maintain backward compatibility possible Follow existing patterns codebase Add tests new functionality bug fixes Never leave trailing whitespace end lines blank lines Always add blank line headings bullet points

title: Copilot Instructions for UtilsGGSV
text: IMPORTANT: adding removing exported functions, update _pkgdown.yml reference index include exported functions.
code: devtools::document()  # Update documentation devtools::test()      # Run tests devtools::check()     # Full package check

title: Copilot Instructions for UtilsGGSV
text: See .github/instructions/ detailed guidelines: r-coding-standards.instructions.md - R code style documentation testing.instructions.md - Test patterns testthat usage package-development.instructions.md - Development workflow

title: Copilot Instructions for UtilsGGSV
text: updating copilot instructions: Keep files 250 lines (max 1000) Use headings, bullets, clear sections Use short, imperative rules long paragraphs Include code examples (correct incorrect patterns) external links - copy info instead vague language - avoid “accurate”, “identify issues” Use applyTo frontmatter topic files path-specific rules

title: UtilsGGSV
text: UtilsGGSV provides ggplot2-based utilities solve two common pain points exploratory data analysis: plot_group_heatmap() — ECDF-percentile heat map showing group’s relative position every variable. plot_group_density() — per-variable density plots per-group overlays (density curves /median lines). plot_group_scatter() — biaxial scatter optional PCA / t-SNE / UMAP projection cluster centroids. plot_group_mst() — minimum-spanning-tree layout coloured ECDF scale heat map. Correlation visualisation — ggcorr() creates paired scatter plots Spearman, Pearson, Kendall, concordance correlation coefficients overlaid formatted table, support log / asinh / scales transformation. Additional helpers round toolkit: axis_limits() — force equal axis limits expand axis coordinates without manually computing values. add_text_column() — place column text annotations consistent relative position regardless underlying axis transformation. get_trans() — retrieve scales transformation name, including higher-root asinh transformations available base scales.

title: UtilsGGSV
text: can install UtilsGGSV GitHub :
code: if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("SATVILab/UtilsGGSV")

title: UtilsGGSV
code: library(UtilsGGSV) library(ggplot2) theme_set(cowplot::theme_cowplot())

title: UtilsGGSV
text: function ggcorr plots correlation coefficients:  can display multiple correlation coefficients:  can compare two groups:  can compare two groups multiple correlation coefficients:  Specific functionality make appropriate plots concordance correlation coefficient available:  Text table can moved around resized:  Finally, text placement kept consistent axes visually transformed:
code: set.seed(3) response_vec_a <- rnorm(5) response_tbl <- data.frame(   group = rep(letters[1:3], each = 5),   response = c(     response_vec_a,     response_vec_a * 1.2 + rnorm(5, sd = 0.2),     response_vec_a * 2 + rnorm(5, sd = 2)   ),   pid = rep(paste0("id_", 1:5), 3) )  ggcorr(   data = response_tbl %>% dplyr::filter(group %in% c("a", "b")),   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid" ) ggcorr(   data = response_tbl %>% dplyr::filter(group %in% c("a", "b")),   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = c("spearman", "pearson") ) ggcorr(   data = response_tbl,   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = "kendall" ) ggcorr(   data = response_tbl,   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = c("spearman", "pearson") ) ggcorr(   data = response_tbl %>% dplyr::filter(group %in% c("a", "b")),   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = "concordance",   abline = TRUE,   limits_equal = TRUE ) ggcorr(   data = response_tbl %>% dplyr::filter(group %in% c("a", "b")),   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = c("spearman", "pearson", "concordance"),   abline = TRUE,   limits_equal = TRUE,   coord = c(0.4, 0.17),   font_size = 3,   skip = 0.04,   pval_signif = 2,   est_signif = 2,   ci_signif = 2 ) ggcorr(   data = response_tbl %>% dplyr::mutate(response = abs(response + 1)^4),   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid",   corr_method = "spearman",   abline = TRUE,   limits_equal = TRUE,   trans = "log10",   skip = 0.06 )

title: UtilsGGSV
text: Fix axis limits equal x- y-axes, /expand axis coordinates. primary use axis_limits forcing x- y-axes limits “automatically” (.e. inspecting ggplot object, thus requiring user manually calculate limits pass ggplot2::expand_limits).
code: data("cars", package = "datasets")  p0 <- ggplot(cars, aes(speed, dist)) +   cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy") +   geom_point() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +   labs(title = "Axes unadjusted") +   labs(x = "Speed", y = "Distance") p1 <- axis_limits(   p = p0,   limits_equal = TRUE ) +   labs(title = "Axes limits equal") p2 <- axis_limits(   p = p0,   limits_expand = list(     x = c(0, 50),     y = c(-10, 200)   ) ) +   labs(title = "Axes limits expanded") cowplot::plot_grid(p0, p1, p2)

title: UtilsGGSV
text: Add column text easily plot, regardless underlying transformation, using add_text_column.  Note add_text_column places text position, regardless underlying transformation.
code: data_mod <- data.frame(x = rnorm(mean = 1, 10)^2) data_mod$y <- data_mod$x * 3 + rnorm(10, sd = 0.5) fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = data_mod) coef_tbl <- coefficients(summary(fit)) results_vec <- c(   paste0(     "Intercept: ",     signif(coef_tbl[1, "Estimate"][[1]], 2),     " (",     signif(coef_tbl[1, 1][[1]] - 2 * coef_tbl[1, 2][[1]], 3),     ", ",     signif(coef_tbl[1, 1][[1]] + 2 * coef_tbl[1, 2][[1]], 3),     "; p = ",     signif(coef_tbl[1, 4][[1]], 3),     ")"   ),   paste0(     "Slope: ",     signif(coef_tbl[2, "Estimate"][[1]], 2),     " (",     signif(coef_tbl[2, 1][[1]] - 2 * coef_tbl[2, 2][[1]], 3),     ", ",     signif(coef_tbl[2, 1][[1]] + 2 * coef_tbl[2, 2][[1]], 3),     "; p = ",     signif(coef_tbl[2, 4][[1]], 3),     ")"   ) ) p <- ggplot(   data = data_mod,   aes(x = x, y = y) ) +   geom_point() +   cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy")  add_text_column(   p = p,   x = data_mod$x,   y = data_mod$y,   text = results_vec,   coord = c(0.05, 0.95),   skip = 0.07 ) p <- p +   scale_y_continuous(     trans = UtilsGGSV::get_trans("asinh")   ) add_text_column(   p = p,   x = data_mod$x,   y = data_mod$y,   text = results_vec,   trans = UtilsGGSV::get_trans("asinh"),   coord = c(0.05, 0.95),   skip = 0.07 )

title: UtilsGGSV
text: plot_cluster_* family functions helps visualise characteristics clusters identified unsupervised learning method.

title: UtilsGGSV
text: function plot_cluster_heatmap creates heat map tile shows percentile median value variable cluster. percentile compared ECDF variable across observations cluster. Clusters variables ordered hierarchical clustering.
code: set.seed(1) cluster_data <- data.frame(   cluster = rep(paste0("C", 1:3), each = 20),   var1 = c(rnorm(20, 2), rnorm(20, 0), rnorm(20, -2)),   var2 = c(rnorm(20, -1), rnorm(20, 1), rnorm(20, 0)) ) plot_cluster_heatmap(cluster_data, cluster = "cluster")

title: UtilsGGSV
text: function plot_cluster_density visualises, variable, cluster’s observations distributed relative overall population. density argument controls shown: "overall" (default, overall density plus cluster median lines), "cluster" (one density curve per cluster), "" (overall density plus per-cluster density curves). showing per-cluster densities, scale argument controls scaling: default ("max_overall") cluster density rescaled maximum equals overall density maximum.
code: set.seed(1) cluster_data <- data.frame(   cluster = rep(paste0("C", 1:3), each = 20),   var1 = c(rnorm(20, 2), rnorm(20, 0), rnorm(20, -2)),   var2 = c(rnorm(20, -1), rnorm(20, 1), rnorm(20, 0)) ) # Default: overall density with cluster median lines plot_cluster_density(cluster_data, cluster = "cluster") #> $var1 #>  #> $var2 # Both overall and per-cluster densities (scaled to overall maximum) plot_cluster_density(cluster_data, cluster = "cluster", density = "both") #> $var1 #>  #> $var2

title: UtilsGGSV
text: function plot_cluster_scatter creates biaxial scatter plot observations coloured cluster median centroids overlaid. two variables supplied defaults PCA projection.  Raw variables can also used directly:
code: set.seed(123) example_data <- data.frame(   cluster = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 20),   var1 = c(rnorm(20, 2), rnorm(20, 0), rnorm(20, -2)),   var2 = c(rnorm(20, -1), rnorm(20, 1), rnorm(20, 0)),   var3 = c(rnorm(20, 1), rnorm(20, -1), rnorm(20, 0)) )  # Default: PCA projection (> 2 numeric variables) plot_cluster_scatter(example_data, cluster = "cluster") #> dim_red automatically set to 'pca' because more than two numeric variables are available. plot_cluster_scatter(   example_data,   cluster = "cluster",   dim_red = "none",   vars = c("var1", "var2") )

title: UtilsGGSV
text: function plot_cluster_mst computes minimum-spanning tree (MST) clusters, using Euclidean distance cluster median profiles. Clusters laid two dimensions via classical multidimensional scaling (MDS). variable, separate plot produced node filled according ECDF-standardised percentile cluster’s median — colour scale used plot_cluster_heatmap. default named list plots returned; supplying n_col n_row returns combined cowplot::plot_grid figure.  Combine grid variable-name labels:
code: set.seed(1) cluster_data <- data.frame(   cluster = rep(paste0("C", 1:3), each = 20),   var1 = c(rnorm(20, 2), rnorm(20, 0), rnorm(20, -2)),   var2 = c(rnorm(20, -1), rnorm(20, 1), rnorm(20, 0)) ) # Default: returns a named list of plots, one per variable plot_list <- plot_cluster_mst(cluster_data, cluster = "cluster") plot_list[["var1"]] plot_cluster_mst(cluster_data, cluster = "cluster", n_col = 2)

title: UtilsGGSV
text: utility function get_trans returns trans objects (implemented scales package) given characters. also adds various higher roots (cubic quartic) adds asinh transformation.
code: get_trans("log10") #> Transformer: log-10 [1e-100, Inf]

what: Add a column of text to a ggplot — add_text_column
title: Add a column of text to a ggplot — add_text_column
text: Adds column text ggplot object, overlaying text specified coordinates customizable transformation, font size, alignment.

title: Add a column of text to a ggplot — add_text_column
code: add_text_column(   p,   x,   y,   trans = "identity",   text,   coord = c(0.05, 0.95),   skip = 0.06,   font_size = 4.2,   hjust = 0,   vjust = 0.5,   ... )

title: Add a column of text to a ggplot — add_text_column
text: p ggplot object. Plot text overlaid onto. x, y numeric vector. min max vectors specify range plot. trans character trans object. Specifies (visual) transformation applied y-axis. Internally passed get_trans. Default "identity". Note transformation applied y-axis (assumption already ). text character vector. element specifies line text. Subsequent elements plotted underneath (assuming skip >= 0). coord numeric vector length two. elements specify x- y-coordinates, respectively, first line text (.e. first element) text. c(0, 0) means bottom-left corner c(1, 1) top-right. problem specifying values outside [0, 1], however. Default c(0.05, 0.95) (.e. towards top-left corner). skip numeric. Proportion y-axis length skip rows. Default 0.06. font_size numeric. Font size. Default 4.2 (roughly size 12). hjust, vjust numeric. Passed hjust vjust parameters geom_text. Defaults 0 0.5, respectively. ... Additional parameters passed geom_text.

title: Add a column of text to a ggplot — add_text_column
text: ggplot object added text.

title: Add a column of text to a ggplot — add_text_column
code: data_mod <- data.frame(x = rnorm(10)) data_mod$y <- data_mod$x * 3 + rnorm(10, sd = 0.5) fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = data_mod) coef_tbl <- coefficients(summary(fit)) results_vec <- c(   paste0(     "Intercept: ", signif(coef_tbl[1, "Estimate"][[1]], 2), " (",     signif(coef_tbl[1, 1][[1]] - 2 * coef_tbl[1, 2][[1]], 3), ", ",     signif(coef_tbl[1, 1][[1]] + 2 * coef_tbl[1, 2][[1]], 3), "; p = ",     signif(coef_tbl[1, 4][[1]], 3), ")"   ),   paste0(     "Slope: ", signif(coef_tbl[2, "Estimate"][[1]], 2), " (",     signif(coef_tbl[2, 1][[1]] - 2 * coef_tbl[2, 2][[1]], 3), ", ",     signif(coef_tbl[2, 1][[1]] + 2 * coef_tbl[2, 2][[1]], 3), "; p = ",     signif(coef_tbl[2, 4][[1]], 3), ")"   ) ) library(ggplot2) library(UtilsGGSV) p <- ggplot(data_mod, aes(x = x, y = y)) + geom_point() add_text_column(   p = p,   x = data_mod$x,   y = data_mod$y,   text = results_vec )   # Works even if y-axis is transformed p <- p + scale_y_continuous(trans = get_trans("asinh")) add_text_column(   p = p,   x = data_mod$x,   y = data_mod$y,   text = results_vec,   trans = "asinh" )

title: Manage axis limits — axis_limits
text: Manage axis limits. Fix axis limits equal x- y-axes, /expand axis coordinates. primary use axis_limits forcing x- y-axes limits "automatically" (.e. inspecting ggplot object, thus requiring user manually calculate limits pass ggplot2::expand_limits).

title: Manage axis limits — axis_limits
code: axis_limits(p, limits_expand = NULL, limits_equal = FALSE)

title: Manage axis limits — axis_limits
text: p object class 'ggplot'. Limits adjusted plot. limits_expand list. NULL, (effectively) passed onto ggplot2::expand_limits ensure certain values included plot (, example, 0 minimum value possible may plotted). named, # nolint must consist one numeric vector force values numeric value # nolint included plot. named, must names x /y, # nolint elements numeric vectors must included plot. limits_equal logical. TRUE, ranges x- y-axes # nolint must equal. Effectively applied expand_grid applied. Default FALSE. # nolint

title: Manage axis limits — axis_limits
code: data("cars", package = "datasets") library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(cars, aes(speed, dist)) +   geom_point()  axis_limits(   p,   limits_equal = TRUE )   # both axes axis_limits(   p,   limits_expand = list(200) )  # x only axis_limits(   p,   limits_expand = list(x = 75) )  # y only axis_limits(   p,   limits_expand = list(y = 200) )  # lower and upper expansion axis_limits(   p,   limits_expand = list(     y = c(-50, 200),     x = c(-10, 75)   ) )   # note that when fixing range and expanding, range is fixed # after expansions are applied, so effectively the larger expansions apply to both. # compare the following output to the previous output: axis_limits(   p,   limits_expand = list(     y = c(-50, 200),     x = c(-10, 75)   ),   limits_equal = TRUE )

what: Merge clusters based on variable bin thresholds — cluster_merge_bin
title: Merge clusters based on variable bin thresholds — cluster_merge_bin
text: Assigns cluster bin variable based cluster's median falls relative provided thresholds. Clusters fall combination bins across variables merged.

title: Merge clusters based on variable bin thresholds — cluster_merge_bin
code: cluster_merge_bin(data, cluster, thresholds)

title: Merge clusters based on variable bin thresholds — cluster_merge_bin
text: data matrix data.frame. Rows observations, columns variables. Must contain columns whose names match thresholds. cluster character vector. Cluster assignment observation. Length must equal nrow(data). thresholds named list. element numeric vector threshold values variable given element name. thresholds define bin boundaries: single threshold t creates two bins (<= t > t), two thresholds create three bins, .

title: Merge clusters based on variable bin thresholds — cluster_merge_bin
text: named list two elements: assign: tibble::tibble() one row per observation columns orig (original cluster label) merged (merged cluster label). label: tibble::tibble() one row per unique original cluster columns orig (original cluster label), level (concise bin-combination label formed joining per-variable bin indices "_") descriptive (human-readable bin-combination description formed joining per-variable range descriptions "; ").

title: Merge clusters based on variable bin thresholds — cluster_merge_bin
code: set.seed(1) mat <- matrix(   c(rnorm(20, 2), rnorm(20, -2)),   ncol = 2,   dimnames = list(NULL, c("var1", "var2")) ) cl <- rep(c("A", "B", "C", "D"), each = 5) thresholds <- list(var1 = 0, var2 = 0) cluster_merge_bin(mat, cl, thresholds) #> $assign #> # A tibble: 20 × 2 #>    orig  merged #>    <chr> <chr>  #>  1 A     2_1    #>  2 A     2_1    #>  3 A     2_1    #>  4 A     2_1    #>  5 A     2_1    #>  6 B     2_1    #>  7 B     2_1    #>  8 B     2_1    #>  9 B     2_1    #> 10 B     2_1    #> 11 C     2_1    #> 12 C     2_1    #> 13 C     2_1    #> 14 C     2_1    #> 15 C     2_1    #> 16 D     2_1    #> 17 D     2_1    #> 18 D     2_1    #> 19 D     2_1    #> 20 D     2_1    #>  #> $label #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   orig  level descriptive         #>   <chr> <chr> <chr>               #> 1 A     2_1   var1 > 0; var2 <= 0 #> 2 B     2_1   var1 > 0; var2 <= 0 #> 3 C     2_1   var1 > 0; var2 <= 0 #> 4 D     2_1   var1 > 0; var2 <= 0 #>

title: Get transformation object — get_trans

title: Get transformation object — get_trans
code: get_trans(trans)

title: Get transformation object — get_trans
text: trans character trans object. class character, converted trans object. Adds "root_cube", "root_fourth", "root_fifth" "asinh" transformations, well "sqrt" transformation allows plotting lines zero. class trans object, returned .

text: trans object corresponding specified transformation.

title: Get transformation object — get_trans
code: x_vec <- seq(1, 5, length.out = 1000) y_vec <- get_trans("root_fifth")$transform(x_vec) plot_tbl <- data.frame(x = x_vec, y = y_vec) library(ggplot2) ggplot(plot_tbl, aes(x, y)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   coord_equal() +   expand_limits(x = 5, y = 5)

what: Plot scatterplot with correlation coefficients and ab-line overlaid — ggcorr
title: Plot scatterplot with correlation coefficients and ab-line overlaid — ggcorr
text: Plots scatterplot various correlation coefficients optional ab-line overlaid.

title: Plot scatterplot with correlation coefficients and ab-line overlaid — ggcorr
code: ggcorr(   data,   corr_method = "spearman",   corr_lab = "auto",   grp,   grp_base = NULL,   grp_x = NULL,   grp_lab = "auto",   y,   id,   est_signif = 3,   ci = TRUE,   pval = TRUE,   ci_signif = 3,   pval_signif = 3,   pval_trunc = 0.001,   label_id = FALSE,   label_id_size = 3,   trans = "identity",   coord = c(0.05, 0.95),   skip = 0.05,   font_size = 4.2,   hjust = 0,   vjust = 0.5,   point_size = 1,   point_alpha = 0.9,   limits_expand = NULL,   limits_equal = FALSE,   legend_title = FALSE,   legend_position = "bottom",   thm = cowplot::theme_cowplot() + theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white")),   grid = cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy"),   grp_to_col = NULL,   abline = FALSE,   smooth = TRUE,   smooth_method = "lm",   smooth_se = FALSE )

title: Plot scatterplot with correlation coefficients and ab-line overlaid — ggcorr
text: data dataframe. Dataframe containing data plotted. corr_method character vector. Correlation method. One c("concordance", "pearson", "spearman", "kendall"). Default "spearman". corr_lab "never"``, "auto"“, "always" named character vector. Whether, , label correlation methods plot. "always" "never", correlation labels always never displayed, respectively. `"auto"`, displayed multiple correlation methods used. displaye dusing `"always"` `"never"`, default labels used. named character vector, names correlation methods values labels, e.g. `c("spearman" = "Spearman", "kendall" = "Kendall")`. Default `"auto"`. correlation methods used. grp character. Name column data specifies variables compare . first level within grp (.e. data[[grp]][1]) level plotted x-axis, unless grp_x set (see ). grp_base character. NULL, correlations level within data[[grp]] levels displayed. NULL, two-way correlations displayed. Default NULL. grp_x character. NULL, level grp plotted along x-axis. Default NULL. grp_lab "always", "auto" "never". Whether label groups compared specific displayed correlation coefficients. "always" "never", groups always never labelled, respectively. "auto", groups labelled multiple groups compared. Default "auto". y character. Name column data specifies values correlated groups. id character. Name column data specifies subject multiple measurements taken. est_signif numeric. Significant digits correlation estimates. Default 3. ci logical. Whether include confidence intervals correlation estimates. Default TRUE. pval logical. Whether include p-values correlation estimates. Default TRUE. ci_signif numeric. Significant digits confidence intervals. Default 3. pval_signif numeric. Significant digits p-values. Default 3. pval_trunc numeric. P-values smaller threshold displayed "< threshold". Default 0.001. label_id logical. TRUE, points labels given data[[id]] displayed using ggrepel::geom_text_repel. Default FALSE. label_id_size numeric. Size labels. Default 3. trans character trans object. Specifies scaling y- x-axes. class character, converted trans object. Adds "root_cube", "root_fourth", "root_fifth" "asinh" transformations, well "sqrt" transformation allows plotting lines zero. class trans object, returned . Default "identity". coord numeric vector. Coordinates text placement first () row results. Specified terms proportion axis length, first value specifying x-axis coordinate second value y-axis coordinate. Default c(0.05, 0.95), places first row top left corner plot. skip numeric. Proportion y-axis move lines text. Default 0.05. font_size numeric. Font size text results table. Default 10. hjust numeric. Horizontal justification text. Default 0. vjust numeric. Vertical justification text. Default 0.5. point_size numeric. Size points. Default 1. point_alpha numeric. Alpha value points. Default 0.9. limits_expand list. Expand axis limits. Default NULL. limits_equal logical. Set axis limits equal. Default FALSE. legend_title logical. FALSE, legend title removed. Default FALSE. legend_position character. Position legend. Default "bottom". NULL, legend's positioning jadjusted. thm ggplot2 theme. Specifies ggplot2 theme. Default cowplot::theme_cowplot(), white plot background (opposed transparent). grid value calling cowplot::background_grid. Specifies display grid lines. NULL, background grid displayed (recommended non-cowplot-themed plots). Default cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy"), displays major grid lines x- y-axes. grp_to_col character vector. Specifies colours elements grp. Elements colours. named, names specify level within grp colours matched. NULL two groups compared, points black. NULL two groups compared, defaults palette Set1 RColorBrewer package. Default NULL. abline logical. TRUE, y=x line plotted colour "gray85". Default TRUE. smooth logical. TRUE, linear regression line plotted. smooth_method character. Smoothing method pass geom_smooth. Default "lm". smooth_se logical. Whether display 95% confidence interval smooth line. Default FALSE.

title: Plot scatterplot with correlation coefficients and ab-line overlaid — ggcorr
code: response_vec_a <- rnorm(5) response_tbl <- data.frame(   group = rep(letters[1:3], each = 5),   response = c(     response_vec_a,     response_vec_a * 1.2 + rnorm(5, sd = 0.2),     response_vec_a * 2 + rnorm(5, sd = 2)   ),   pid = rep(paste0("id_", 1:5), 3) ) library(UtilsGGSV) ggcorr(   data = response_tbl,   grp = "group",   y = "response",   id = "pid" )

what: Plot density of variable values with per-group overlays — plot_group_density
title: Plot density of variable values with per-group overlays — plot_group_density
text: variable, plots kernel density estimates per-group overlays (density curves /median lines). Returns named list ggplot2 objects single faceted plot.

title: Plot density of variable values with per-group overlays — plot_group_density
code: plot_group_density(   .data,   group,   vars = NULL,   col_clusters = NULL,   n_col = NULL,   n_row = NULL,   density = "both",   scale = "max_overall",   scales = "free_y",   expand_coord = NULL,   exclude_min = "no",   rug = NULL,   density_overall_weight = NULL,   bandwidth = "hpi_1",   na_rm = TRUE,   font_size = 14,   thm = cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_size = font_size) + ggplot2::theme(plot.background =     ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA), panel.background =     ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)),   grid = cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy") )  plot_cluster_density(.data, cluster, ...)

title: Plot density of variable values with per-group overlays — plot_group_density
text: .data data.frame. Rows observations. Must contain column identifying group membership columns variable values. group character. Name column .data identifies group membership. vars character vector NULL. Names columns .data use variables. NULL, columns except group used. Default NULL. col_clusters named character vector NULL. Per-group colours. Names match group labels. NULL (default), default ggplot2 colour scale used. n_col integer NULL. Number columns passed ggplot2::facet_wrap. supplied (n_row supplied) single faceted plot returned instead list. Default NULL. n_row integer NULL. Number rows passed ggplot2::facet_wrap. supplied (n_col supplied) single faceted plot returned instead list. Default NULL. density character. density display. One "" (default: overall density curve plus per-cluster density curves), "overall" (overall density curve plus cluster median lines), "cluster" (one density curve per cluster, coloured cluster). See Details. scale character. scale per-cluster density curves. relevant density "cluster" "". One "max_overall" (default: cluster density rescaled maximum equals maximum overall density, keeping y-axis values comparable overall density), "max_cluster" (rescaling; y-axis determined tallest curve), "free" (rescaling; equivalent "max_cluster"). scales character. scales argument passed ggplot2::facet_wrap faceted plot requested. Default "free_y" x-axis shared across panels. expand_coord numeric vector named list NULL. Expands x-axis limits include given values. plain numeric vector applied every variable. named list (names = variable names, values = numeric vectors) applies expansion per variable. faceted plot requested via n_col/n_row named list provided, warning issued expand_coord ignored (incompatible faceting). Default NULL. exclude_min character. Whether exclude observations whose value equals minimum density median calculations. Options : "" (default, exclusion), "overall" (exclude observations whose value equals global minimum across variables), "variable" (variable, exclude observations whose value equals variable's minimum). rug character NULL. Controls rug added density. NULL (default): per-cluster rug density "cluster" "", overall rug density "overall". "cluster": per-cluster rug, coloured cluster. "overall": overall rug cluster colouring. See Details. density_overall_weight character NULL. Controls weighting overall density density "overall" "". NULL (default): overall density estimated observations pooled together. "even": overall density computed equal-weight average per-cluster kernel densities, preventing larger clusters dominating density estimate. Ignored density "cluster". bandwidth character positive numeric. Bandwidth used per-cluster kernel density estimation. One "hpi_1" (default), "hpi_0", "SJ", positive number. See Details. na_rm logical. Whether remove NA values variable computing densities medians. TRUE (default), NA values removed message issued showing many removed per variable. FALSE, NA values passed directly stats::density(), strip warning. font_size numeric. Font size passed cowplot::theme_cowplot. Default 14. thm ggplot2 theme object NULL. Default cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_size = font_size) white plot background. Set NULL apply theme adjustment. grid ggplot2 panel grid NULL. Default cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy"). Set NULL grid. cluster character. Name column .data identifies group membership. Alias group parameter. ... Additional arguments passed plot_group_density().

title: Plot density of variable values with per-group overlays — plot_group_density
text: named list ggplot2 objects (one per variable) neither n_col n_row specified. single ggplot2 object facet_wrap panels n_col n_row specified.

title: Plot density of variable values with per-group overlays — plot_group_density
text: density argument controls shown: "" (default): overall density curve plus one density curve per group, coloured group. Group curves scaled according scale argument. "overall": overall density observations vertical line per group group's median value. "cluster": one density curve per group, coloured group.

title: Plot density of variable values with per-group overlays — plot_group_density
text: rug added default. rug argument controls data shows: NULL (default): per-group rug density "cluster" "", overall rug density "overall". "cluster": per-group rug, coloured group. "overall": overall rug (group colouring).

title: Plot density of variable values with per-group overlays — plot_group_density
text: bandwidth argument controls per-group bandwidth selection (used per-group density curves even-weighted overall density): "hpi_1" (default): ks::hpi(x, deriv.order = 1) — plug-bandwidth based first derivative, less sensitive group size SJ. "hpi_0": ks::hpi(x, deriv.order = 0) — plug-bandwidth based density . "SJ": Sheather-Jones bandwidth via stats::bw.SJ(). positive numeric value: use value bandwidth directly. bandwidth estimation fails, warning issued default (stats::bw.nrd0) bandwidth used fallback.

title: Plot density of variable values with per-group overlays — plot_group_density
text: default function returns named list ggplot2 objects, one per variable. n_col n_row supplied plots instead combined single faceted ggplot2 object via facet_wrap.

title: Plot density of variable values with per-group overlays — plot_group_density
code: set.seed(1) .data <- data.frame(   group = rep(paste0("C", 1:3), each = 20),   var1 = c(rnorm(20, 2), rnorm(20, 0), rnorm(20, -2)),   var2 = c(rnorm(20, -1), rnorm(20, 1), rnorm(20, 0)) ) # Default: overall + per-group density curves plot_list <- plot_group_density(.data, group = "group")  # Overall density with group median lines only plot_group_density(.data, group = "group", density = "overall") #> $var1  #>  #> $var2  #>   # Per-group density curves only plot_group_density(.data, group = "group", density = "cluster") #> $var1  #>  #> $var2  #>   # Even-weighted overall density plot_group_density(   .data, group = "group", density_overall_weight = "even" ) #> $var1  #>  #> $var2  #>   # Faceted plot with 2 columns plot_group_density(.data, group = "group", n_col = 2)

what: Plot heat map of scaled variable values per group — plot_group_heatmap
title: Plot heat map of scaled variable values per group — plot_group_heatmap
text: Creates heat map tile shows scaled summary variable group. scaling method controlled scale_method parameter. default (scale_method = "ecdf"), tile shows percentile group's median value compared empirical cumulative distribution function (ECDF) variable across observations belonging group. Groups variables ordered along axes via hierarchical clustering.

title: Plot heat map of scaled variable values per group — plot_group_heatmap
code: plot_group_heatmap(   .data,   group,   vars = NULL,   scale_method = "ecdf",   palette = "bipolar",   col = c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B"),   col_positions = "auto",   white_range = c(0.4, 0.6),   na_rm = TRUE,   font_size = 14,   thm = cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_size = font_size) + ggplot2::theme(plot.background =     ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA), panel.background =     ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)),   grid = cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy"),   show_values = FALSE,   values_format = NULL,   values_col = "black",   values_size = 3 )  plot_cluster_heatmap(.data, cluster, ...)

title: Plot heat map of scaled variable values per group — plot_group_heatmap
text: .data data.frame. Rows observations. Must contain column identifying group membership columns variable values. group character. Name column .data identifies group membership. vars character vector NULL. Names columns .data use variables. NULL, columns except group used. Default NULL. scale_method character. Method used scale variable values colouring cells. One "ecdf" (default), "zscore", "raw", "minmax", "minmax_var". "ecdf": cell shows percentile cluster's median value compared observations outside cluster (empirical CDF). Fill values [0, 1] legend uses percent labels. "zscore": cell shows z-score cluster's median relative observations variable ((median - mean) / sd). Fill values unbounded. "raw": cell shows raw median value. Fill values unbounded. "minmax": cell shows cluster median scaled [0, 1] using global minimum maximum across observations variables. Fill values [0, 1] legend uses percent labels. "minmax_var": cell shows cluster median scaled [0, 1] using minimum maximum observations within variable separately. Fill values [0, 1] legend uses percent labels. palette character NULL. Named colour palette continuous fill scale. NULL, overrides col col_positions. Available palettes: "bipolar" (default, blue-white-red), "alarm" (green-white-red, good--bad), "accessible" (blue-white-orange, colour-blind-safe diverging), "heat" (light-yellow dark-red, sequential), "sky" (white navy, sequential). Set NULL use col col_positions directly. col character vector. Colours used fill tiles, ordered low high values. Default c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B") (blue, white, red). number colours (>= 2) accepted. Ignored palette NULL. col_positions numeric vector "auto". Positions ([0, 1]) colour col placed fill scale. Must length col, sorted ascending order, first value 0 last value 1. "auto" (default) col exactly three colours scale_method = "ecdf", middle colour stretched white_range (current default behaviour). "auto" cases colours evenly spaced 0 1. Ignored palette NULL. white_range numeric vector length 2. range positions (0-1 scale) middle colour stretched. used col exactly three colours, scale_method = "ecdf", col_positions = "auto". Also applied diverging palette presets (col_positions = "auto"). Default c(0.4, 0.6). na_rm logical. Whether remove NA values computing per-cluster statistics. TRUE (default), NA values removed message issued showing many removed per variable. FALSE, NA values passed : tile fill values NA (rendered grey default) variable non-missing observations cluster. font_size numeric. Font size passed cowplot::theme_cowplot. Default 14. thm ggplot2 theme object NULL. Default cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_size = font_size) white plot background. Set NULL apply theme adjustment. grid ggplot2 panel grid NULL. Default cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy"). Set NULL grid. show_values logical. Whether overlay median value cluster-variable combination text label tile. Default FALSE. values_format function NULL. function takes numeric vector returns character vector formatted labels. Applied per-cluster median values show_values = TRUE. NULL, values formatted three significant figures using formatC(x, digits = 3, format = "g"). Default NULL. values_col character. Colour overlaid text labels. Default "black". values_size numeric. Font size (ggplot2 units) overlaid text labels. Default 3. cluster character. Name column .data identifies group membership. Alias group parameter. ... Additional arguments passed plot_group_heatmap().

title: Plot heat map of scaled variable values per group — plot_group_heatmap
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot heat map of scaled variable values per group — plot_group_heatmap
code: set.seed(1) .data <- data.frame(   group = rep(paste0("C", 1:3), each = 20),   var1 = c(rnorm(20, 2), rnorm(20, 0), rnorm(20, -2)),   var2 = c(rnorm(20, -1), rnorm(20, 1), rnorm(20, 0)) ) plot_group_heatmap(.data, group = "group")  plot_group_heatmap(.data, group = "group", show_values = TRUE)  plot_group_heatmap(.data, group = "group", palette = "alarm")

what: Plot minimum-spanning tree of groups with per-variable node colouring — plot_group_mst
title: Plot minimum-spanning tree of groups with per-variable node colouring — plot_group_mst
text: Computes minimum-spanning tree (MST) groups, distance two groups Euclidean distance median variable profiles. MST built full pairwise Euclidean distance matrix (.e. fully connected undirected weighted graph), matching approach used FlowSOM (BuildMST). node layout determined shared across per-variable plots. Two layout algorithms supported via layout_algorithm: "kamada-kawai" (default): uses Kamada-Kawai force-directed algorithm (igraph::layout_with_kk) MST graph, matching FlowSOM visualisation style. "mds": uses classical multidimensional scaling (stats::cmdscale) full Euclidean distance matrix. variable, separate plot produced group node filled according ECDF-standardised percentile group's median value variable — scaling used plot_group_heatmap(). node border label colour encode group identity can overridden via col_clusters. default function returns named list ggplot2 objects, one per variable. n_col n_row supplied plots combined single figure using cowplot::plot_grid, variable names labels.

title: Plot minimum-spanning tree of groups with per-variable node colouring — plot_group_mst
code: plot_group_mst(   .data,   group,   vars = NULL,   layout_algorithm = c("kamada-kawai", "mds"),   coord_equal = TRUE,   suppress_axes = NULL,   col_clusters = NULL,   palette = "bipolar",   col = c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B"),   col_positions = "auto",   white_range = c(0.4, 0.6),   na_rm = TRUE,   n_col = NULL,   n_row = NULL,   label_x = 0,   label_y = 1,   hjust = -0.5,   vjust = 1.5,   font_size = 14,   thm = cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_size = font_size) + ggplot2::theme(plot.background =     ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA), panel.background =     ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)),   grid = cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy") )  plot_cluster_mst(.data, cluster, ...)

title: Plot minimum-spanning tree of groups with per-variable node colouring — plot_group_mst
text: .data data.frame. Rows observations. Must contain column identifying group membership columns variable values. group character. Name column .data identifies group membership. vars character vector NULL. Names columns .data use variables. NULL, columns except group used. Default NULL. layout_algorithm character. Layout algorithm positioning nodes. One "kamada-kawai" (default) "mds". "kamada-kawai" uses Kamada-Kawai force-directed algorithm MST graph via igraph::layout_with_kk, matching FlowSOM visualisation style. "mds" uses classical multidimensional scaling full distance matrix via stats::cmdscale. coord_equal logical. Whether enforce equal visual scaling axes (one unit x-axis equals one unit y-axis) via ggplot2::coord_equal(). Default TRUE. suppress_axes logical NULL. Whether suppress axis text, ticks, lines, titles. NULL (default), value inherited coord_equal — axes suppressed equal scaling active. col_clusters named character vector NULL. Per-cluster colours applied node borders text labels. Names match cluster labels. NULL (default), colour-blind-friendly palette ("Paired") used. palette character NULL. Named colour palette continuous node fill scale. NULL, overrides col col_positions. Available palettes: "bipolar" (default, blue-white-red), "alarm" (green-white-red, good--bad), "accessible" (blue-white-orange, colour-blind-safe diverging), "heat" (light-yellow dark-red, sequential), "sky" (white navy, sequential). Set NULL use col col_positions directly. col character vector. Colours used fill nodes, ordered low high values. Default c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B") (blue, white, red). number colours (>= 2) accepted. Ignored palette NULL. col_positions numeric vector "auto". Positions ([0, 1]) colour col placed fill scale. Must length col, sorted ascending order, first value 0 last value 1. "auto" (default) col exactly three colours, middle colour stretched white_range. "auto" cases colours evenly spaced 0 1. Ignored palette NULL. white_range numeric vector length 2. range positions (0-1 scale) middle colour stretched. used col exactly three colours col_positions = "auto". Also applied diverging palette presets. Default c(0.4, 0.6). na_rm logical. Whether remove NA values computing per-cluster medians ECDF percentiles. TRUE (default), NA values removed message issued showing many removed per variable. FALSE, NA values passed : node fill values NA (rendered grey default) variable non-missing observations cluster. n_col integer NULL. Number columns passed cowplot::plot_grid. supplied (n_row supplied) single combined figure returned instead list. Default NULL. n_row integer NULL. Number rows passed cowplot::plot_grid. supplied (n_col supplied) single combined figure returned instead list. Default NULL. label_x numeric. x position plot labels within panel grid mode. Passed cowplot::plot_grid. Default 0. label_y numeric. y position plot labels within panel grid mode. Passed cowplot::plot_grid. Default 1. hjust numeric. Horizontal justification plot labels grid mode. Passed cowplot::plot_grid. Default -0.5. vjust numeric. Vertical justification plot labels grid mode. Passed cowplot::plot_grid. Default 1.5. font_size numeric. Font size passed cowplot::theme_cowplot. Default 14. thm ggplot2 theme object NULL. Default cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_size = font_size) white plot background. Set NULL apply theme adjustment. grid ggplot2 panel grid NULL. Default cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy"). Set NULL grid. cluster character. Name column .data identifies group membership. Alias group parameter. ... Additional arguments passed plot_group_mst().

title: Plot minimum-spanning tree of groups with per-variable node colouring — plot_group_mst
text: named list ggplot2 objects (one per variable) neither n_col n_row specified. cowplot::plot_grid figure n_col n_row specified.

title: Plot minimum-spanning tree of groups with per-variable node colouring — plot_group_mst
code: set.seed(1) .data <- data.frame(   group = rep(paste0("C", 1:3), each = 20),   var1 = c(rnorm(20, 2), rnorm(20, 0), rnorm(20, -2)),   var2 = c(rnorm(20, -1), rnorm(20, 1), rnorm(20, 0)) ) # Default: Kamada-Kawai layout, returns a named list of plots plot_list <- plot_group_mst(.data, group = "group")  # MDS layout plot_group_mst(.data, group = "group", layout_algorithm = "mds") #> $var1  #>  #> $var2  #>   # Combined grid with 2 columns plot_group_mst(.data, group = "group", n_col = 2)

what: Biaxial scatter plot with group medians overlaid — plot_group_scatter
title: Biaxial scatter plot with group medians overlaid — plot_group_scatter
text: Creates biaxial scatter plot observations colored group assignment. Median group centroids overlaid large points labeled group names. plot can use either raw variables (specified user) dimensionality reduction components axes.

title: Biaxial scatter plot with group medians overlaid — plot_group_scatter
code: plot_group_scatter(   .data,   group,   dim_red = NULL,   vars = NULL,   dim_red_args = list(),   point_col_var = NULL,   point_col = NULL,   palette = "bipolar",   col = c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B"),   col_positions = "auto",   white_range = c(0.4, 0.6),   na_rm = TRUE,   point_size = 2,   point_alpha = 0.65,   centroid_size = 3,   label_size = 4,   label_offset = 0.3,   ggrepel = TRUE,   font_size = 14,   x_lab = NULL,   y_lab = NULL,   show_legend = TRUE,   thm = cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_size = font_size) + ggplot2::theme(plot.background =     ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA), panel.background =     ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)),   grid = cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy") )  plot_cluster_scatter(.data, cluster, ...)

title: Biaxial scatter plot with group medians overlaid — plot_group_scatter
text: .data data.frame. Rows observations. Must contain column identifying group membership numeric variables. group character. Name column .data identifies group membership. dim_red character NULL. Dimensionality reduction method: one "none", "pca", "tsne", "umap". NULL, auto-selects "none" exactly 2 numeric vars available, otherwise "pca". vars character vector NULL. Names numeric columns .data use plot reduction. NULL, uses numeric columns except group point_col_var. dim_red_args named list. Additional arguments passed dimensionality reduction function, overriding defaults set plot_cluster_scatter. dim_red = "pca" passed stats::prcomp() (default: scale. = TRUE); dim_red = "tsne" Rtsne::Rtsne() (defaults: dims = 2, perplexity auto-computed, check_duplicates = FALSE, pca = FALSE); dim_red = "umap" umap::umap(). data argument always set internally overridden. Ignored dim_red = "none". Default list(). point_col_var character NULL. Column use point colour mapping. Default cluster. point_col named vector NULL. Custom colours discrete point_col_var (named level). Ignored continuous point_col_var (use col instead). palette character NULL. Named colour palette continuous point_col_var colour scale. NULL, overrides col col_positions. Available palettes: "bipolar" (default, blue-white-red), "alarm" (green-white-red, good--bad), "accessible" (blue-white-orange, colour-blind-safe diverging), "heat" (light-yellow dark-red, sequential), "sky" (white navy, sequential). Set NULL use col col_positions directly. Ignored point_col_var discrete. col character vector. Colours used continuous point_col_var colour scale, ordered low high values. Default c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B") (blue, white, red). number colours (>= 2) accepted. Ignored point_col_var discrete palette NULL. col_positions numeric vector "auto". Positions ([0, 1]) colour col placed colour scale. Must length col, sorted ascending, first value 0 last value 1. "auto" (default) col exactly three colours, middle colour stretched white_range. "auto" cases colours evenly spaced 0 1. Ignored point_col_var discrete palette NULL. white_range numeric vector length 2. range positions (0-1 scale) middle colour stretched. used col exactly three colours col_positions = "auto". Also applied diverging palette presets. Default c(0.4, 0.6). Ignored point_col_var discrete. na_rm logical. Whether remove observations missing values variables used plot axes (dimensionality reduction, applicable). TRUE (default), missing observations removed message issued showing many. FALSE dim_red "none", error raised missing values found (dimensionality reduction algorithms handle ). FALSE dim_red = "none", observations missing axis values silently dropped ggplot2. point_size numeric. Size observation points. Default 2. point_alpha numeric. Alpha transparency observation points legend guide. Default 0.65. centroid_size numeric. Size centroid points. Default 3. label_size numeric. Font size centroid labels. Default 4. label_offset numeric. Label repulsion padding centroid labels cm. Default 0.3. ggrepel logical. Use ggrepel::geom_text_repel centroid labels. Default TRUE. font_size numeric. Font size passed cowplot::theme_cowplot. Default 14. x_lab character NULL. Label x axis; default uses reduction variable names. y_lab character NULL. Label y axis; default uses reduction variable names. show_legend logical. Whether show legend. Default TRUE. Set FALSE hide legend, centroid labels may suffice. thm ggplot2 theme object NULL. Default cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_size = font_size) white background. grid ggplot2 layer NULL. Background grid added plot. Default cowplot::background_grid(major = "xy"). Set NULL suppress grid. cluster character. Name column .data identifies group membership. Alias group parameter. ... Additional arguments passed plot_group_scatter().

title: Biaxial scatter plot with group medians overlaid — plot_group_scatter
code: set.seed(1) .data <- data.frame(   group = rep(paste0("C", 1:3), each = 20),   var1 = c(rnorm(20, 2), rnorm(20, 0), rnorm(20, -2)),   var2 = c(rnorm(20, -1), rnorm(20, 1), rnorm(20, 0)),   var3 = c(rnorm(20, 1), rnorm(20, -1), rnorm(20, 0)) ) plot_group_scatter(.data, group = "group") #> dim_red automatically set to 'pca' because more than two numeric variables are available.  plot_group_scatter(.data, group = "group", dim_red = "none", vars = c("var1", "var2"))  plot_group_scatter(.data, group = "group", show_legend = FALSE) #> dim_red automatically set to 'pca' because more than two numeric variables are available.  # Pass extra arguments to the dim-red function, e.g. disable scaling in PCA: plot_group_scatter(.data, group = "group", dim_red = "pca",                      dim_red_args = list(scale. = FALSE))

what: Save plot in multiple formats using cowplot::save_plot — save_plot
title: Save plot in multiple formats using cowplot::save_plot — save_plot
text: Wrapper around cowplot::save_plot save plots multiple formats.

title: Save plot in multiple formats using cowplot::save_plot — save_plot
code: save_plot(filename, plot, device = c("png", "pdf"), ...)

title: Save plot in multiple formats using cowplot::save_plot — save_plot
text: filename character. Name plot (including directory, save working directory). plot gg object. Output either ggplot2::ggplot cowplot::plot_grid. device character vector. Devices save (.e. plot format). Default c("png", "pdf"). ... passed cowplot::save_plot.

title: Save plot in multiple formats using cowplot::save_plot — save_plot
text: invisible(filename).

title: UtilsGGSV (development version)
text: Fix devtools::check() WARNING: documented ... argument plot_cluster_* alias functions. Fix devtools::check() NOTE: add ^issues$ .Rbuildignore. Fix devtools::check() NOTE: import utils::modifyList plot_group_scatter(). Add Depends: R (>= 3.5.0) suppress serialised-object build warning.

title: UtilsGGSV 0.9.0
text: Add plot_group_* family primary API (plot_group_density(), plot_group_heatmap(), plot_group_mst(), plot_group_scatter()), plot_cluster_* functions kept backwards-compatible aliases. Rename core .data parameter uniformly across plot_group_* plot_cluster_* functions (data functions). Update @param vars documentation refer groups rather clusters throughout.

title: UtilsGGSV 0.8.0
text: Add palette parameter plot_cluster_heatmap(), plot_cluster_density(), plot_cluster_mst(), plot_cluster_scatter() five built-colour-blind-friendly presets: "bipolar" (default), "alarm", "accessible", "heat", "sky". Add na_rm parameter plot_cluster_* functions; NA values now removed default informative message. Rename data parameter .data plot_cluster_* functions avoid masking rlang::.data pronoun. Add multiple scale methods plot_cluster_heatmap() via new scale_method parameter: "ecdf" (default), "zscore", "raw", "minmax", "minmax_var". Add show_values, values_format, values_col, values_size parameters plot_cluster_heatmap() overlaying median tile labels.

title: UtilsGGSV 0.7.6
text: Patch release: internal consistency improvements minor documentation fixes following 0.7.5 input-validation work.

title: UtilsGGSV 0.7.5
text: Add input validation plot_cluster_* functions; informative error messages now raised invalid .data, cluster, vars arguments. Make parameter names consistent across plot_cluster_density(), plot_cluster_heatmap(), plot_cluster_mst(), plot_cluster_scatter().

title: UtilsGGSV 0.7.4
text: Add bandwidth parameter plot_cluster_density() "hpi_1" new default (plug-bandwidth via ks::hpi()). Change default value density parameter plot_cluster_density() "overall" "". Restructure plot_cluster_density() documentation clarity.

title: UtilsGGSV 0.7.3
text: Add rug layer plot_cluster_density(), controlled new rug parameter. Add automatic coercion cluster column factor plot_cluster_density(). Add even-weighting option overall density estimate plot_cluster_density() via density_overall_weight parameter.
